title: Silene rubricalyx
taxonomy: Silene rubricalyx is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Caryophyllaceae. This plant belongs to the genus Silene, which is a group of plants commonly known as catchflies or campions. The Caryophyllaceae family includes over 2,000 species of plants, many of which are native to temperate regions. Silene rubricalyx is a rare and endemic species found in the southwestern United States. The plant's classification as a Caryophyllaceae member reflects its characteristics and shared traits with other members of this family.

intro: Silene rubricalyx, also known as Red Catchfly, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Caryophyllaceae family, characterized by its bright red flowers and distinctive seed pods. The plant has been found to possess potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of conditions such as arthritis and protect against cell damage.
